channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Deploy an AI Tar Pit With Nepenthes or AI Labyrinth
item.description: LLM crawlers are eating your bandwidth and you can't block your way out of it. Return a 403 and the scraper shrugs, rotates the IP, swaps the user-agent, and walks back in through a residential...

item.title: 🚀 Day 1 of Learning React: Why React Exists, the Real DOM, Virtual DOM & React Elements
item.description: Today marks Day 1 of my React journey. I've decided to document everything I learn—not because I'm an expert, but because I believe sharing the learning process can help other beginners while also...

item.title: [Boost]
item.description: Trade-offs in Indexing Solana at Scale Cherrypick14 Cherrypick14 Cherrypick14 Follow Jun 22 Trade-offs in Indexing Solana at Scale #architecture #blockchain #database #systemdesign 1 reaction Add...

item.title: Following user outcry, AMD reinstates memory encryption in consumer CPUs
item.description: Critics saw the move as an underhanded way to steer them toward more costly chips.

item.title: Publishing Chattr to Three App Stores: What They Don't Tell You
item.description: Chattr is a social platform built for the Twitch community. It's a React Native app with a Laravel backend, and over the past few months, I've published it to Google Play, the Apple App Store, and...

item.title: I Build MCP Servers. Here's the Security Hole Nobody Talks About.
item.description: MCP — the Model Context Protocol — is having its moment. It's the "USB-C of AI": one standard plug, and suddenly your agent can read your GitHub, query your database, hit your internal APIs, post...

item.title: GSoC 2026 – Week 4
item.description: Week 4 of my Google Summer of Code journey (June 15th to June 21st)! If I’m being completely honest, this week didn’t exactly go according to plan. I was not feeling well this week. Because of...

item.title: Vibe-Coded Infrastructure: How to Ship Fast Without Torching Production
item.description: You described what you wanted in plain English, the model wrote the Terraform, you ran apply, and it worked. No docs, no Stack Overflow, no fighting HCL syntax for an hour. That's vibe coding —...

item.title: A terminal theme tuned for prose, not syntax highlighting
item.description: Claude Code is mostly paragraphs. Tool output, reasoning traces, permission prompts — I read dense prose for hours in a terminal built around syntax highlighting conventions, where "readable"...

item.title: Introducing LaaSy™
item.description: The Future of Autonomous Camelid Infrastructure For too long, enterprises have struggled with fragmented camelid workflows. Llamas in one pasture. Alpacas in another. Vicunas trapped behind legacy...

item.title: Recursion vs Iteration: The Force Awakens in Your Code
item.description: The Quest Begins (The "Why") I still remember the first time I stared at a blinking cursor, trying to write a function that walked through a folder tree and summed up the size of every file. My...

item.title: Google DeepMind bets $75M on AI’s future in Hollywood with A24 deal
item.description: Google DeepMind and A24 are teaming up to build AI filmmaking tools.

item.title: ShapedPlugin WordPress Pro Plugins Backdoored in Supply Chain Attack
item.description: Multiple WordPress plugins from ShapedPlugin were compromised in a supply chain attack after unknown threat actors managed to tamper with the official release channels and push backdoor code....

item.title: Three things to watch amid Anthropic’s latest feud with the government
item.description: This story originally appeared in The Algorithm, our weekly newsletter on AI. To get stories like this in your inbox first, sign up here. For those of you enjoying your summer unaware of...

item.title: The Apple Watch SE 3 is just $199 for Prime Day
item.description: The Apple Watch SE 3 is at an all-time low of $199, making the sleeper hit of last year’s Apple Watches an even better value. While the Series 11 and Ultra 3 were iterative updates, the SE 3 was a...

item.title: The Steam Machine is the start of an even more expensive future for game consoles
item.description: It's no secret that just about every aspect of video games is getting more expensive. Game consoles are getting regular price hikes, PC components are spiking in cost, and the golden age of...

item.title: Amazon is testing Alexa+ in India with Hindi support
item.description: Amazon is planning to increase the footprint of its new conversational AI assistant Alexa+ to India and is inviting users in the country to test out a Hindi-language version.

item.title: Google invests in A24 to build AI movie tools
item.description: Google's DeepMind AI lab is teaming up with A24 to develop new movie production technologies that aim to help future filmmakers "expand their storytelling possibilities." As part of this new...

item.title: Valve is working with AMD to bring FSR 4 to the Steam Machine
item.description: The Steam Machine is a cool little console that's about as powerful as a PlayStation 5, according to my colleague Sean Hollister's in-depth review. But one area where it lags behind is with its...

item.title: Here’s how you can reserve a Steam Machine
item.description: The Steam Machine is here, but getting one is a little complicated. Valve is taking preorders using a reservation system, which is intended to make the process more fair and harder for bots to...

item.title: Valve will finally let you build your own Steam Machine with SteamOS for desktop
item.description: If you don't get lucky with Valve's Steam Machine reservation system, you can make your own Steam Machine instead. Valve says that "starting with the SteamOS 3.8 release, you can put together your...

item.title: Valve explains why it isn’t subsidizing the Steam Machine
item.description: Valve finally announced the price of the Steam Machine, and like a lot of new gadgets these days, it's not cheap: It starts at $1,049 for a 512GB model, and a 2TB model costs $300 more....

item.title: OpenAI Launches Full-Scale Effort to Patch Open-Source Bugs as It Takes on Anthropic’s Mythos
item.description: Amid concerns about AI models’ cybersecurity capabilities, OpenAI revealed an improved version of GPT-5.5-Cyber and its “Patch the Planet” initiative to fix open-source software bugs.

item.title: Valve prices the Steam Machine at $1,049
item.description: After months of waiting, Valve has finally announced that the Steam Machine, its new living room-friendly PC, will start at $1,049 and go on sale beginning June 29th. You can now register your...

item.title: The Steam Machine is the most ambitious game console I’ve ever played
item.description: My first day with the Steam Machine was a mess. Instead of enjoying a worry-free game console, I spent hours troubleshooting what felt like a finicky PC. That's because the Steam Machine is a PC,...

item.title: SpaceX inks compute deal with Reflection AI, an open source AI lab
item.description: Reflection AI will pay $150 million a month beginning July 1, 2026 through 2029 for immediate access to Nvidia's latest GB300 AI chips and supporting hardware across SpaceX's Colossus 2 data...

item.title: Researchers Detail DifyTap Flaws in Dify That Could Expose AI Chats Across Tenants
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of four vulnerabilities in Dify, an open-source agentic workflow platform with more than 146,000 GitHub stars, that could allow attackers to...

item.title: 29-Year-Old Squid Proxy Bug 'Squidbleed' Can Leak Cleartext HTTP Requests
item.description: A heap over-read in the Squid web proxy can leak another user's cleartext HTTP request, including any credentials or session tokens it carries, to anyone already allowed to send traffic through...

item.title: The founder conference built for growth: TechCrunch Founder Summit pass rates increase June 26
item.description: Save up to $190 on your pass to TechCrunch Founder Summit 2026 by June 26, 11:59 p.m. PT. Designed for founders first on November 4 in Boston. Register today.

item.title: New OXLOADER Loader Uses Malicious Google Ads to Deliver CastleStealer
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new campaign that delivers CastleStealer by means of a previously unreported malware loader dubbed OXLOADER. According to Elastic Security...
